docs.title: Ollie's Single Cell Analysis for the Romagnani lab (OSCAR)
docs.text: OSCAR is a comprehensive pipeline for single-cell sequencing analysis, developed for the Romagnani lab at Charité. It provides tools and workflows for processing CITE-seq, DOGMA-seq, ASAP-seq, and Tapestri experiments.

docs.title: 1. Clone the Repository
docs.text:
cd $HOME/work/bin/
git clone https://github.com/ollieeknight/OSCAR

docs.title: 2. Download Apptainer Images
docs.text:
There are two Apptainer images:

  • oscar-count.sif - For counting steps
  • oscar-qc.sif - For post-counting quality control
mkdir -p ${TMPDIR}/OSCAR
apptainer pull library://romagnanilab/oscar/oscar-count:latest --dir ${TMPDIR}/OSCAR/
apptainer pull library://romagnanilab/oscar/oscar-qc:latest --dir ${TMPDIR}/OSCAR/

docs.title: 3. Reference Genomes
docs.text: Reference genomes are also required. Build steps can be found under the reference/ directory.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Automated Metadata Generation - Create standardized metadata files for your experiments
  • Feature Barcode References - Generate barcode references for Cell Ranger, Kallisto, and Tapestri
  • Streamlined Processing - Bash scripts to automate the entire workflow
  • Quality Control - Comprehensive QC steps built into the pipeline

docs.title: Supported Assays
docs.text:
• CITE-seq - Cellular Indexing of Transcriptomes and Epitopes by Sequencing
  • DOGMA-seq - DNA, Oligonucleotide, Gene expression, and Methylation Analysis
  • ASAP-seq - ATAC with Select Antigen Profiling by sequencing
  • Tapestri - Single-cell DNA and protein sequencing

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
For questions, suggestions, or issues:

  • Email: oliver.knight@charite.de
  • GitHub Issues: Report a bug or request a feature

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide will walk you through setting up OSCAR for single-cell sequencing analysis.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Access to a Linux/Unix system (BIH cluster recommended)
  • Apptainer/Singularity installed
  • Basic command-line knowledge

docs.title: 1. Clone the Repository
docs.text:
cd $HOME/work/bin/
git clone https://github.com/ollieeknight/OSCAR
cd OSCAR

docs.title: 2. Download Container Images
docs.text: OSCAR uses two Apptainer containers for different stages of the analysis:

docs.title: oscar-count.sif
docs.text:
Used for alignment and counting: 

mkdir -p ${TMPDIR}/OSCAR
apptainer pull library://romagnanilab/oscar/oscar-count:latest --dir ${TMPDIR}/OSCAR/

docs.title: oscar-qc.sif
docs.text:
Used for quality control and downstream analysis: 

apptainer pull library://romagnanilab/oscar/oscar-qc:latest --dir ${TMPDIR}/OSCAR/

docs.title: 3. Set Up Reference Genomes
docs.text: Reference genomes are required for alignment. Build scripts are provided in the reference/ directory:

docs.title: Human Reference (GRCh38)
docs.text:
cd reference/
bash human.sh

docs.title: Mouse Reference (GRCm39)
docs.text:
cd reference/
bash mouse.sh

docs.title: Kallisto Index (for ASAP-seq)
docs.text:
cd reference/
bash human_kallisto.sh

docs.title: 1. Create Project Directory
docs.text:
PROJECT_ID="my_experiment"
DIR_PREFIX="${HOME}/scratch/ngs"
mkdir -p ${DIR_PREFIX}/${PROJECT_ID}
cd ${DIR_PREFIX}/${PROJECT_ID}

docs.title: 2. Prepare Metadata File
docs.text:
Create a metadata.csv file or use the Metadata Generator tool.

Example metadata structure: 

assay,experiment_id,historical_number,replicate,modality,chemistry,index_type,index,species,n_donors,adt_file
CITE,EXP001,H001,R1,RNA,3prime,dual,SI-TT-A1,Human,1,adt_totalseq_a.csv

docs.title: 3. Add FASTQ Files
docs.text:
Place your FASTQ files in: 

${DIR_PREFIX}/${PROJECT_ID}/fastq/

docs.title: Step 1: Process Metadata
docs.text:
bash ${HOME}/work/bin/OSCAR/bash/01_process_metadata.sh \
    --project-id ${PROJECT_ID} \
    --dir-prefix ${DIR_PREFIX}

docs.title: Step 2: Process FASTQ Files
docs.text:
bash ${HOME}/work/bin/OSCAR/bash/02_fastq.sh \
    --project-id ${PROJECT_ID} \
    --dir-prefix ${DIR_PREFIX}

docs.title: Step 3: Process Libraries
docs.text:
bash ${HOME}/work/bin/OSCAR/bash/03_process_libraries.sh \
    --project-id ${PROJECT_ID} \
    --dir-prefix ${DIR_PREFIX}

docs.title: Step 4: Count
docs.text:
bash ${HOME}/work/bin/OSCAR/bash/04_count.sh \
    --project-id ${PROJECT_ID} \
    --dir-prefix ${DIR_PREFIX}

docs.title: Step 5: Quality Control
docs.text:
bash ${HOME}/work/bin/OSCAR/bash/05_quality_control.sh \
    --project-id ${PROJECT_ID} \
    --dir-prefix ${DIR_PREFIX}

docs.title: Custom Settings
docs.text:
Edit bash/config.sh to customize:

  • Reference genome paths
  • Container image locations
  • Default parameters

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
export OSCAR_HOME="${HOME}/work/bin/OSCAR"
export OSCAR_IMAGES="${TMPDIR}/OSCAR"

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
FASTQ files not found

Ensure FASTQ files follow the naming convention expected by your sequencing platform.

Memory errors

Increase memory allocation in your job submission script.

Checking logs

Log files are created in ${DIR_PREFIX}/${PROJECT_ID}/logs/

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Metadata Generator - Create properly formatted metadata
  • Feature Barcode Generator - Generate ADT/HTO reference files
  • Functions Reference - Detailed script documentation

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
For help or questions:

  • Email: oliver.knight@charite.de
  • GitHub Issues: Submit an issue

docs.title: OSCAR Functions Reference
docs.text: This page provides detailed documentation for all OSCAR bash scripts.

docs.title: 01_process_metadata.sh
docs.text:
Description: This script processes the metadata file for a given project. It reads the metadata file, determines the run type, and creates index files for each library based on the metadata.

Inputs:

  • --project-id: The project ID
  • --dir-prefix: The directory prefix (default: ${HOME}/scratch/ngs)
  • --metadata-file-name: The metadata file name (default: metadata.csv)

Outputs: Index files for each library in the indices folder

Example: 

bash 01_process_metadata.sh \
    --project-id MY_PROJECT \
    --dir-prefix ${HOME}/scratch/ngs \
    --metadata-file-name metadata.csv

docs.title: 02_fastq.sh
docs.text:
Description: This script performs FASTQ demultiplexing for a given project. It uses the index files created by 01_process_metadata.sh to run the cellranger command and generate FASTQ files.

Inputs:

  • --project-id: The project ID
  • --dir-prefix: The directory prefix (default: ${HOME}/scratch/ngs)
  • --metadata-file-name: The metadata file name (default: metadata.csv)

Outputs: FASTQ files and quality control reports in the fastq folder

Example: 

bash 02_fastq.sh \
    --project-id MY_PROJECT \
    --dir-prefix ${HOME}/scratch/ngs

docs.title: 03_process_libraries.sh
docs.text:
Description: This script processes the libraries for a given project. It reads the metadata file, determines the run type, and handles different modes (GEX or ATAC) to prepare the libraries for counting.

Inputs:

  • --project-id: The project ID
  • --dir-prefix: The directory prefix (default: ${HOME}/scratch/ngs)
  • --gene-expression-options: Options for gene expression processing (comma-separated)
  • --vdj-options: Options for VDJ processing (comma-separated)
  • --adt-options: Options for ADT processing (comma-separated)
  • --metadata-file-name: The metadata file name (default: metadata.csv)

Outputs: Prepared libraries for counting in the libraries folder

Example: 

bash 03_process_libraries.sh \
    --project-id MY_PROJECT \
    --dir-prefix ${HOME}/scratch/ngs \
    --gene-expression-options "expect-cells=10000"

docs.title: 04_count.sh
docs.text:
Description: This script submits counting jobs for each library in a given project. It reads the library files, determines the run type, and submits the appropriate counting jobs to SLURM.

Inputs:

  • --project-id: The project ID
  • --dir-prefix: The directory prefix (default: ${HOME}/scratch/ngs)
  • --metadata-file-name: The metadata file name (default: metadata.csv)

Outputs: Counting job submissions and logs in the outs folder

Example: 

bash 04_count.sh \
    --project-id MY_PROJECT \
    --dir-prefix ${HOME}/scratch/ngs

docs.title: 05_quality_control.sh
docs.text:
Description: This script performs quality control for a given project. It checks the necessary OSCAR containers, submits jobs for ambient RNA removal, genotyping, and other quality control tasks.

Inputs:

  • --project-id: The project ID
  • --dir-prefix: The directory prefix (default: ${HOME}/scratch/ngs)
  • --metadata-file-name: The metadata file name (default: metadata.csv)

Outputs: Quality control job submissions and logs in the outs folder

Example: 

bash 05_quality_control.sh \
    --project-id MY_PROJECT \
    --dir-prefix ${HOME}/scratch/ngs

docs.title: Common Options
docs.text:
All scripts support the following common options:

Option Description Default --project-id Unique identifier for your project Required --dir-prefix Base directory for analysis ${HOME}/scratch/ngs --metadata-file-name Name of metadata CSV file metadata.csv

docs.title: Workflow Order
docs.text:
The scripts should be run in numerical order:

  1. 01_process_metadata.sh - Parse metadata and create index files
  2. 02_fastq.sh - Demultiplex and generate FASTQ files
  3. 03_process_libraries.sh - Prepare libraries for counting
  4. 04_count.sh - Run counting (Cell Ranger/Kallisto)
  5. 05_quality_control.sh - Perform QC and filtering

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Parallel Processing

Most scripts can handle multiple libraries automatically and submit parallel jobs to SLURM.

Check Logs

Always check log files in the logs/ directory for any errors or warnings.

Resuming Failed Jobs

If a job fails, you can typically re-run just that step without starting over.

docs.title: Cell Ranger
docs.text: 10x Genomics. (2024). Cell Ranger Software Suite.

docs.title: Cell Ranger ATAC
docs.text: 10x Genomics. (2024). Cell Ranger ATAC Software Suite.

docs.title: FastQC
docs.text: Andrews, S. (2010). FastQC: A Quality Control Tool for High Throughput Sequence Data. https://github.com/s-andrews/FastQC

docs.title: MultiQC
docs.text: Ewels, Philip, et al. (2016). MultiQC: summarize analysis results for multiple tools and samples in a single report. Bioinformatics, 32(19), 3047-3048. https://github.com/MultiQC/MultiQC

docs.title: CellBender
docs.text: Fleming, Stephen J., et al. (2023). Unsupervised removal of systematic background noise from droplet-based single-cell experiments using CellBender. Nature Methods, 20(9), 1323-1335. https://github.com/broadinstitute/CellBender

docs.title: cellsnp-lite
docs.text: Huang, Xianjie, and Yuanhua Huang. (2021). Cellsnp-lite: an efficient tool for genotyping single cells. Bioinformatics, 37(23), 4569-4571. https://github.com/single-cell-genetics/cellsnp-lite

docs.title: Vireo
docs.text: Sinha, R., et al. (2020). Vireo: a scalable approach to analyze single-cell RNA-seq data. Bioinformatics, 36(14), 4247-4249. https://github.com/single-cell-genetics/vireo

docs.title: AMULET
docs.text: Thibodeau, Asa, et al. (2021). AMULET: a novel read count-based method for effective multiplet detection from single nucleus ATAC-seq data. Genome Biology, 22(1), 1-19. https://github.com/UcarLab/AMULET

docs.title: mgatk
docs.text: Lareau, Caleb A., et al. (2021). Massively parallel single-cell mitochondrial DNA genotyping and chromatin profiling. Nature Biotechnology, 39(4), 451-461. https://github.com/caleblareau/mgatk

docs.title: KITE
docs.text: Gehring, Jase, et al. (2020). Highly multiplexed single-cell RNA-seq by DNA oligonucleotide tagging of cellular proteins. Nature Biotechnology, 38(1), 35-38. https://github.com/pachterlab/kite

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use OSCAR in your research, please cite:

Knight, O. (2025). OSCAR: Ollie's Single Cell Analysis for the Romagnani lab.
GitHub repository: https://github.com/ollieeknight/OSCAR

docs.title: Interactive Tools
docs.text: OSCAR provides two interactive web tools to help you generate properly formatted files for your single-cell analysis workflows.

docs.title: Metadata Generator
docs.text:
Create standardized metadata CSV files for your experiments. This tool guides you through all the required fields and ensures your metadata is correctly formatted for OSCAR processing.

Use this tool to:

  • Generate metadata files for single-cell experiments
  • Ensure consistent formatting across projects
  • Validate required fields before processing

docs.title: Feature Barcode Generator
docs.text:
Generate feature barcode reference files for Cell Ranger, Kallisto (KITE), and Tapestri workflows. Select from TotalSeq antibody panels and export in the correct format for your platform.

Use this tool to:

  • Create ADT/HTO reference files
  • Search BioLegend TotalSeq panels
  • Export in Cell Ranger, Kallisto, or Tapestri format
